notes.concept_name: Consumer Exploitation and Unfair Trade Practices
notes.summary: Consumer exploitation occurs when sellers use unfair methods such as overcharging, selling defective goods, adulteration, hoarding, black marketing and misleading advertisements. These practices violate consumer rights and create imbalance in the market.
notes.importance: Builds awareness about market malpractices and the need for legal protection.

notes.summary: Consumers are protected by several rights including Right to Safety, Right to Information, Right to Choose, Right to Seek Redressal and Right to Consumer Education. These rights ensure fairness, transparency and accountability in the market.
notes.importance: Helps students understand legal safeguards available to consumers.
quick_facts: Right to Safety protects from hazardous goods
Right to Information ensures product details
Right to Choose allows freedom of selection
Right to Seek Redressal ensures compensation

notes.summary: The Consumer Protection Act provides a legal framework to protect consumers against exploitation. It establishes consumer courts and provides a simple and low-cost mechanism for complaint redressal.
notes.importance: Highlights the role of law in protecting consumer interests.

notes.summary: Consumer disputes are handled through a three-tier system: District Forum, State Commission and National Commission. The level of court depends on the value of the claim.
notes.importance: Helps understand the structured legal process for grievance redressal.

notes.summary: Certification marks like ISI, Agmark, Hallmark and FSSAI indicate product quality and safety standards. These marks help consumers make informed choices and avoid substandard products.
notes.importance: Promotes safe purchasing decisions and quality assurance.

notes.summary: Along with rights, consumers have responsibilities such as checking product details, demanding bills, being aware of quality marks and filing complaints against unfair practices.
notes.importance: Encourages responsible and informed consumer behavior.
